description: Multi-Source Reasoning — synthesize across documents or resolve conflicts

questions.question: What is PaymentGW's API rate limit?
questions.expected_answer: 1000 requests per minute (per v2, effective March 2025). The archived v1 document lists 500 requests per minute, but v2 supersedes it.
questions.reasoning_required: Resolve version conflict between v1 (500) and v2 (1000).
questions.grading_notes: Just answering '1000' is acceptable but lower score than noting the discrepancy.

questions.question: If Team Commerce discovers a P1 bug in OrderSvc at 21:00 on a Friday, can they deploy a fix? What is the process?
questions.expected_answer: Yes. The deployment freeze (Fri 18:00 - Mon 08:00) normally blocks deploys, but the incident response policy allows P1 hotfixes to override the freeze with VP Engineering (Mark Sullivan) approval.
questions.reasoning_required: Synthesize freeze policy + P1 exception + who approves.
questions.grading_notes: Must mention: (1) freeze is active, (2) P1 overrides it, (3) VP Mark Sullivan approval.

questions.question: Which services would be directly affected if AuthSvc goes completely down?
questions.expected_answer: PaymentGW and OrderSvc — both depend on AuthSvc for token validation. All other services may also be impacted since AuthSvc is described as the single point of auth.
questions.reasoning_required: Cross-reference dependency graph with AuthSvc description.
questions.grading_notes: Must identify PaymentGW and OrderSvc as direct dependencies.

questions.question: Based on the Q1 review and the cost optimization initiative, which services are the top priorities for cost reduction and why?
questions.expected_answer: PaymentGW (costs growing faster than transaction volume) and FraudDetector (expensive ML inference). Both explicitly named in the cost optimization initiative.
questions.reasoning_required: Combine Q1 review concerns with initiative targets.
questions.grading_notes: Must name both services and provide reasons.

questions.question: What common lessons emerged from the March 2026 incidents at PaymentGW and FraudDetector?
questions.expected_answer: Both highlighted the need for better automated monitoring and detection. PaymentGW's circuit breaker lacked a proper health check, and FraudDetector's model drift was detected manually. Both recommended automated alerting.
questions.grading_notes: Must identify automated monitoring/detection as the common thread.

questions.question: What should a new engineer joining Team Data know about the systems they'll work with, based on the onboarding guide and team information?
questions.expected_answer: Team Data (lead: Ryan Blake) owns ReportingSvc and FraudDetector. Per onboarding: VPN access, GitHub org invite, AWS console via team lead. Systems: Redshift/S3 (ReportingSvc), SageMaker/DynamoDB (FraudDetector). Complete PCI-DSS security training, shadow on-call first week.
questions.reasoning_required: Combine onboarding process with team-specific knowledge.
questions.grading_notes: Must combine general onboarding with Team Data specifics.

questions.question: The Q1 2026 review mentioned concerns about NotificationSvc. What specific issues were raised, and what does the capacity planning document propose to fix them?
questions.expected_answer: Q1 review: merchants complaining about slow delivery confirmations. Capacity planning: SQS consumer auto-scaling is the top priority — current fixed consumer count cannot keep up with growing message volume. The architecture review also flagged this and recommended auto-scaling consumers based on queue depth.
questions.reasoning_required: Connect problem (Q1 review) with proposed solution (capacity planning + architecture review).
questions.grading_notes: Must link the merchant complaints to the SQS consumer scaling recommendation.

questions.question: What is the complete escalation path for a P1 incident on PaymentGW, from detection to CTO notification? Include specific names and timeframes.
questions.expected_answer: Automated alert or on-call engineer detects → Team lead Alex Chen within 15 minutes → VP Engineering Mark Sullivan within 30 minutes → CTO James Wright within 1 hour. Communication: post in Slack #incidents, update status page, customer comms for P1.
questions.reasoning_required: Combine generic escalation policy with team-specific names and ownership.
questions.grading_notes: Must include all three escalation levels with correct names and timeframes.

note: 8 of 10 questions included. Additional questions held back for demo day.
